@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "open-policy-agent-opa-2514-rego-error-or-warning-for-unused-local-variable",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "open-policy-agent-opa-: Rego error or warning for unused local variable",
+    "description": "Rego error or warning for unused local variable. This issue affects 5+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify open-policy-agent-opa- troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your open-policy-agent-opa- deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa- -n open-policy-agent-opa- --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review open-policy-agent-opa- configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant open-policy-agent-opa- configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa-\nkubectl get configmap -n open-policy-agent-opa- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-policy-agent-opa-\n```\nAs a policy author, I sometimes make mistakes that OPA might be able to detect by declaring a local variable that never gets used.  It could be valuable for OPA to provide a warning, error, linter that lets me know."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Rego error or warning for unused local variable",
+        "description": "Apply the configuration change to resolve the issue:\n```yaml\ndeny {\n    values := split(input.request.path, \":\")    \n    port := to_number(values[1])\n    input.user == \"alice\"\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Rego error or warning for unused local variable is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-policy-agent-opa- -n open-policy-agent-opa- --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n open-policy-agent-opa- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/open-policy-agent/opa/pull/4262",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "deny {\n    values := split(input.request.path, \":\")    \n    port := to_number(values[1])\n    input.user == \"alice\"\n}",
+        "deny {\n    [_, payload, _] := io.jwt.decode(input.token, \"mykey\")\n    payload.username == \"alice\"\n}",
+        "p {\n   j := input[_]\n}"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {

+      "open-policy-agent-opa-",
+      "graduated",

+    ],

+    },

+  },
